question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a valid base32 alias that has no corresponding entity in the database to trigger a 'No account found for the given ID' 404 error, and use this to enumerate which aliases correspond to valid accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the endpoint returns 200 with an empty allowances list for a valid account with no NFT allowances, versus 404 for a non-existent account, to perform account existence enumeration across the entire account ID space?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with account.id=gte:0.0.1&account.id=lte:0.0.1 (a single-point range) combined with token.id=gte:0.0.1 to observe whether the Bound.hasLowerAndUpper() optimization correctly collapses this to an equality check, and whether the resulting query is different from account.id=eq:0.0.1?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with account.id=gt:0.0.1&account.id=lt:0.0.3 (a range that includes only 0.0.2) to observe whether the Bound correctly handles exclusive boundaries and whether the resulting query matches only the expected single value?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with account.id=gt:0.0.X&account.id=lt:0.0.X+1 (an empty range where no integer exists between X and X+1) to observe whether the service correctly returns an empty result or whether it executes an unnecessary database query?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with the {id} path variable as a numeric ID that is valid but belongs to a contract account rather than a user account, and observe whether the NFT allowances for contract accounts are returned, leaking contract-level allowance data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with the {id} path variable as a numeric ID that belongs to a deleted account, and observe whether the service returns the historical NFT allowances for the deleted account, leaking data that should no longer be accessible?
